rationale.root_cause: It correctly identifies the memcpy call using sizeof(*structCharVoid) (entire struct) to copy into charFirst, overflowing the 16-byte buffer and overwriting voidSecond/voidThird. It explains the concrete overflow mechanism at the FLAW site around the bad memcpy.
rationale.local_fix: It specifies changing the memcpy size to sizeof(structCharVoid->charFirst), which prevents overwriting the following fields. This matches the required proximate fix.
rationale.global_fix: It suggests strlcpy/strncpy and coding standards, but does not propose a bounded field-copy helper or typed buffer wrapper that derives the destination size to prevent confusing struct size with field size. Therefore it does not meet the stricter global-fix criterion.
